WOW.name: Woolworths Group

n1.name: Managed Turnaround
n1.desc: New leadership executes turnaround; margins stabilize

n2.name: Structural Margin Erosion
n2.desc: Price investment and regulatory pressure permanently compress margins

n3.name: Regulatory Squeeze
n3.desc: ACCC/FGCC action intensifies, constraining pricing power

n4.name: Competitive Disruption
n4.desc: Aldi and independents take meaningful share

WOW.analysis: Your thesis supports N2 and N3 -- the downside scenarios. You believe margin pressure is structural, not cyclical, and regulatory headwinds are underappreciated. This puts you against consensus which prices a Bardwell turnaround (N1).

CSL.name: CSL Limited

n1.name: Plasma Recovery & Growth
n1.desc: Collections normalize; Behring margins expand; Vifor synergies realized

n2.name: Collection Shortfall
n2.desc: US plasma collections fail to recover to target levels

n3.name: Behring Margin Compression
n3.desc: Pricing pressure and input costs squeeze Behring profitability

n4.name: Competitive Threat
n4.desc: Biosimilar competition emerges faster than expected

CSL.analysis: Your thesis strongly supports N1 -- the base case recovery. You believe plasma collections are normalizing and Behring margins will expand. You're aligned with consensus. Watch N2 risks.

XRO.name: Xero Limited

n1.name: AI-Led Growth Recovery
n1.desc: AI investments drive subscriber growth; churn reduces

n3.name: Execution Failure
n3.desc: Management fails to deliver on AI promises; growth stalls

n4.name: Competitive Disruption
n4.desc: QuickBooks or new entrant disrupts with superior AI

XRO.analysis: Your thesis supports N1 -- the turnaround narrative. However, you contradict N3 and N4, representing 50% of probability. High reward if right; significant risk if execution fails.

WTC.name: WiseTech Global

n2.name: Growth Deceleration
n2.desc: Customer acquisition slows; revenue growth moderates

n3.name: Governance/Execution Risk
n3.desc: Founder departure or governance issues impair execution

WTC.analysis: Your thesis is ⚠️ PUSHING UPHILL. You don't align clearly with N1, yet contradict N2, N3, N4 (70% combined). Requires growth recovery AND governance concerns proving overblown.

WDS.name: Woodside Energy

n1.name: Energy Transition Delayed
n1.desc: Fossil demand persists longer; LNG prices remain elevated

n2.name: Managed Decline
n2.desc: Orderly transition; stable cash flows; disciplined capex

n4.name: Execution Failure
n4.desc: Scarborough delays; cost overruns; production misses

WDS.analysis: Your thesis supports N2 -- the managed decline scenario. You expect orderly cash flow generation through transition. Watch N1 (upside) and N3 (downside) scenarios.

MQG.name: Macquarie Group

n1.name: Green Energy Leadership
n1.desc: Green investment bank thesis succeeds; asset realizations strong

n2.name: Commodities Normalization
n2.desc: Commodity earnings normalize; offset by green growth

n3.name: Asset Realization Shortfall
n3.desc: Green asset sales disappoint; valuation gap persists

n4.name: Competitive Pressure
n4.desc: Traditional banks compete effectively on green finance

MQG.analysis: Your thesis strongly supports N1 -- the green energy bank narrative. You believe Macquarie can pivot successfully. Watch N3 risk on asset realizations.

DRO.name: Droneshield

n1.name: Mass Adoption
n1.desc: Government contracts scale rapidly; commercial adoption accelerates

n2.name: Defense Budget Dependence
n2.desc: Revenue tied to lumpy defense procurement

n3.name: Competitive Threat
n3.desc: Larger defense primes enter; pricing pressure emerges

n4.name: Technology Obsolescence
n4.desc: New counter-drone tech renders products obsolete

DRO.analysis: Your thesis supports N2 but acknowledges N3 risks. Droneshield operates in a niche vulnerable to larger competitors. High conviction required given N3/N4 probability (60%).

PME.name: Pro Medicus

n1.name: Continued Dominance
n1.desc: Visage AI maintains technical lead; contract renewals at higher rates

n2.name: Growth Normalization
n2.desc: Growth moderates from exceptional to strong levels

n3.name: Competitive Entry
n3.desc: Big Tech or med-tech enters radiology AI effectively

PME.analysis: Your thesis strongly supports N1 -- the continued dominance scenario. High conviction in technical moat. Watch N3 (competition) as main risk.

GYG.name: Guzman y Gomez

n1.name: Successful Scale-Up
n1.desc: Store rollout succeeds; unit economics improve at scale

n2.name: Growth vs Profitability Tension
n2.desc: Expansion costs weigh; path to profit unclear

n4.name: Execution Failure
n4.desc: Store openings miss targets; franchisee issues emerge

GYG.analysis: Your thesis aligns with N2 -- the growth vs profitability tension. You believe expansion will strain economics. This is the base case for recent IPOs in competitive markets.

GMG.name: Goodman Group

n1.name: Logistics Megatrend
n1.desc: E-commerce growth sustains; development pipeline delivers

n4.name: Interest Rate Sensitivity
n4.desc: Higher rates impair valuations and development returns

GMG.analysis: Your thesis supports N1 -- the logistics megatrend. You believe e-commerce tailwinds persist. Watch N4 (rates) and N3 (oversupply) as cyclical risks.

SIG.name: Sigma Healthcare

n1.name: Chemist Warehouse Synergies
n1.desc: Merger delivers cost synergies; market position strengthens

n2.name: Integration Challenges
n2.desc: Merger execution proves difficult; synergies delayed

n3.name: Regulatory Block
n3.desc: ACCC blocks or conditions merger heavily

SIG.analysis: Your thesis supports N1 -- the merger synergy story. You believe Sigma+Chemist Warehouse creates value. Key risk is N2 (integration execution).

FMG.name: Fortescue

n1.name: Green Hydrogen Success
n1.desc: Fortescue Future Industries becomes viable business

n2.name: Iron Ore Cash Machine
n2.desc: Core iron ore generates cash; green projects scaled back

n3.name: Iron Ore Price Collapse
n3.desc: Chinese demand weakness crushes iron ore prices

n4.name: Green Capital Destruction
n4.desc: Hydrogen investments destroy value with no returns

FMG.analysis: Your thesis aligns with N2 -- the cash machine scenario. You view green hydrogen as optionality, not core value. Watch N3 (China risk) as main downside.
